body_txt: Slothfulness , like any other vice, can be detrimental to those guilty of it. Also, like any other vice, some people choose to indulge in it even if common sense would tell them it's a bad idea. The consequences of this can vary, with financial hardship, injury, and death being some of the more common possibilities. Since there are many ways to be lazy as well as many ways to be stupid, there are naturally many ways for someone to be both. When it comes to reading and doing research, lazy people might often neglect doing such tasks and end up missing out on important information at a crucial time. When it comes to tedious but necessary work, lazy people will sometimes cut corners or neglect said tasks to their own or others' detriment. If a lazy person is part of a team, their slothfulness can potentially make them a liability to said team, which sometimes leads to them being kicked out. Super-Trope to Laborious Laziness (in which a character's attempts to get out of doing work result in them putting in more work). See also Lust Makes You Dumb , Fury-Fueled Foolishness , Impatience Makes You Dumb , and Greed Makes You Dumb for other vices that can lead to characters abandoning their common sense. Can overlap with Lethal Negligence if someone's laziness leads to other people dying or getting hurt, or Arrogance Breeds Laziness if someone's arrogance causes them to be stupid and lazy. Some Ambitious, but Lazy characters can suffer from this flaw, as their laziness can be a hindrance to their ambitions. Someone who has laziness as a Fatal Flaw will sometimes be guilty of this trope. Contrast Dumb, but Diligent , for when a character makes up for their stupidity with diligence. This trope can overlap with Brilliant, but Lazy if a character's laziness actually hampers their brilliance. Usually has nothing to do with Sluggish Sloths .
